explanation: Using block values (block.timestamp, block.number, block.prevrandao, blockhash) with the modulo operator as a randomness source is not random at all: every one of these inputs is either publicly predictable or directly influenceable by the block producer. Miners and validators can bias or precompute the outcome, and other contracts can read the same values in the same block.
impact: An attacker or block producer can predict or manipulate the "random" result to always win lotteries, mint rare NFTs, or drain prize pools, at essentially no cost beyond ordinary block production.
